title: The 10 Highest-Rated Movies of 2025, According to Rotten Tomatoes
text:
Highest-rated movies of 2025 on Rotten Tomatoes (critical consensus):

Top Rankings:

#1: On Becoming a Guinea Fowl (100%). A Zambian drama directed by Rungano Nyoni about a young girl who discovers her uncle's body and uncovers family secrets. The film maintained a perfect rating throughout 2025.

#2: No Other Choice (100%). Park Chan-wook's dark comedy satire starring Lee Byung-hun, exploring corporate struggles through a protagonist's violent descent during job-market desperation.

#3: Eephus (100%). Described as a bittersweet sports comedy with a heart of gold, this film follows New England rec-league teams playing their final game before their field closes.

#4: Souleymane's Story (100%). A French drama depicting an immigrant's challenging asylum application process and daily struggles in Paris.

#5: Deaf President Now! (100%). A documentary recounting a largely overlooked 1988 student protest at Gallaudet University regarding presidential selection.

#6-10: Additional highly-rated films include Come See Me in the Good Light (100%), The Secret Agent (99%), The Perfect Neighbor (99%), Caught by the Tides (99%), and Sister Midnight (98%).

Note: While these films received critical acclaim on Rotten Tomatoes, they represent a distinct category from the year's highest-grossing commercial films, which tend to be blockbuster franchises and animated features targeting wider audiences.
